source_standard.document_title: NRL Expansion Project - Specification for Piping Stress Analysis
source_standard.basis_note: Logic extracted for web-app upfront screening only. Final stress analysis shall be performed by competent piping stress engineer using applicable project codes, standards, line list, equipment data, vendor data, and approved project specifications.

intended_use.purpose: Show upfront stress-analysis criticality, consult warnings, special-service warnings, and interface requirements while the engineer calculates minimum required pipe thickness.
intended_use.important_limitation: This JSON does not calculate minimum wall thickness. The source document itself states that verification of pipe and fitting thickness due to internal pressure is outside the scope of the piping stress-analysis specification.
intended_use.recommended_app_behavior: Run this screening after the user enters pipe size, service, design temperature, design pressure, pressure class, material, connected equipment, fluid/service flags, and special conditions. Display the highest required analysis level and all triggered warnings before accepting the selected pipe schedule.

global_messages.thickness_outside_stress_spec: Pipe thickness due to internal pressure is outside this stress-analysis specification. Calculate minimum required pipe thickness separately as per applicable piping code, then use this screening to identify stress-analysis, support, flexibility, nozzle-load, dynamic, wind, seismic, flange-leakage, and consultation requirements.
global_messages.minimum_thickness_not_final_pipe_accepta: Minimum pressure thickness alone is not final pipe acceptability. Pipe routing, supports, flexibility, nozzle loads, dynamic loads, wind, seismic, flange leakage, corrosion, testing, and vendor limits may govern final pipe specification or support design.
global_messages.consult_stress_engineer: Consult Piping Stress Engineer before finalizing pipe schedule, routing, supports, or nozzle connection.
global_messages.consult_mechanical: Consult Mechanical / Equipment Engineer for nozzle allowable loads, nozzle stiffness, vendor allowable reactions, equipment movements, and local stress checks.
global_messages.consult_process_hse: Consult Process / HSE Design for surge lines, slug/surge forces, two-phase-flow conditions, PSV reaction forces, and special operating cases.
global_messages.consult_civil_structural: Consult Civil / Structural Engineer for support loads, anchor loads, underground soil model, structure interface, wind/seismic support feasibility, and settlement inputs.
global_messages.consult_vendor: Consult Vendor for equipment allowable loads, actuator weights, package limits, pulsation study, expansion-joint data, spring support data, and equipment displacements.

required_inputs.description: Nominal pipe size used for screening. Use NPS/diameter equivalent consistently.

computed_inputs.default_basis: Ambient temperature for flexibility analysis = 21 degC unless project-specific value is provided.

level_1.label: Level 1 - Visual Inspection
level_1.description: Approximate visual inspection of routing and supporting for lines clearly having adequate flexibility. Detailed CAESAR II analysis is not required by this level.
level_1.web_app_output: Level 1: Visual inspection may be acceptable, subject to engineering judgment and project approval.

level_2.label: Level 2 - Simplified CAESAR II Analysis
level_2.description: Simplified computer analysis using CAESAR II. CAESAR II report to be submitted for Owner/PMC review where applicable.
level_2.web_app_output: Level 2: Simplified CAESAR II stress analysis required. Consult Piping Stress Engineer.

level_3.label: Level 3 - Detailed CAESAR II Analysis
level_3.description: Detailed computer analysis using CAESAR II to meet applicable code requirements such as ASME B31.1/B31.3. CAESAR II report to be submitted for Owner/PMC review where applicable.
level_3.web_app_output: Level 3: Detailed CAESAR II stress analysis required. Do not finalize pipe schedule/routing without Piping Stress Engineer review.

evaluation_logic.rule_selection_method: Evaluate all applicable table rules and special-service rules. Select the highest numeric stress level triggered. Always display every triggered warning and consult message.
evaluation_logic.temperature_band_boundary_policy: For implementation, use lower level up to and including the stated boundary temperature delta; use the next higher level above that boundary. Example: <=260 degC uses the lower band, >260 degC uses the next band.
evaluation_logic.small_bore_policy: The stress criticality table mainly starts above 1.5 inch. For NPS <= 1.5 inch, do not auto-clear. Apply special-service rules and show a warning to consult Stress Engineer where service, equipment, vibration, temperature, PSV, slug/surge, or safety criticality exists.
evaluation_logic.multi_category_policy: A line may match more than one category, such as stainless steel plus rotating equipment plus high pressure. The app shall not average categories. It shall use the highest triggered analysis level and show all reasons.

applicable_code_rules.applicable_code_basis: ASME B31.3 Process Piping for process/utility piping unless a more specific project, statutory, or licensor requirement governs.
warnings: Design temperature and pressure shall be taken from line list/design basis. Allowable stress and stress limits shall follow ASME B31.3.

applicable_code_rules.applicable_code_basis: IBR / ASME B31.1 for Steam and BFW piping.
warnings: Lines > 2 inch subjected to IBR regulations are Level 2 minimum.
Consult with your Engineering Department.

applicable_code_rules.applicable_code_basis: ASME B31.3 Chapter IX high-pressure piping definition/check applies.
warnings: Piping 900# and above shall be defined in accordance with Chapter IX of ASME B31.3.
Consult Piping Stress Engineer.

stress_critical_line_list_rules.applicable_when: connected_equipment_type in ['rotating_machine', 'sensitive_equipment', 'centrifugal_pump', 'compressor', 'turbine'] or special_flags contains any of ['plate_fin_exchanger', 'cold_box']

additional_warnings: For tank nozzles, consider radial movement, downward rotation due to bulging, and vertical settlement.
Use of expansion joints shall be avoided as far as possible. Pressure thrust and equipment/support reactions must be verified. EJMA requirements apply.

stress_critical_line_list_rules.category_name: High pressure, Class 600 lb and above
additional_warnings: If pressure class is 900# or above, ASME B31.3 Chapter IX high-pressure piping requirements shall be considered.

stress_critical_line_list_rules.applicable_when: service_code == 'cryogenic' or design_temperature_c < 0 or special_flags contains 'cryogenic'
additional_warnings: For low-temperature carbon steel below -45 degC, impact test requirement and stress ratio verification shall be checked with Materials group.
Vendor shall perform analog/pulsation study. Maintain natural frequency requirements from vendor. Independent supports from grade are preferred for reciprocating equipment piping.

stress_critical_line_list_rules.applicable_when: connected_equipment_type in ['heater', 'furnace', 'compressor', 'turbine'] or special_flags contains any of ['heater_transfer_line', 'to_from_heater', 'to_from_steam_generator']
additional_warnings: All process lines to/from heaters and steam generators are critical category type K. Transfer lines from reactors to heaters are prone to vibration. Spring supports should be avoided as much as possible. Minimum acceptable frequency for transfer lines is 7 Hz.
Aboveground piping connected to underground piping requires underground piping to be included in the stress model up to sufficient extent. Soil model from Civil is required.

stress_critical_line_list_rules.category_name: All other types except noted above

special_service_override_rules.name: Hydrogen high-pressure line
warnings: Hydrogen lines 4 inch and above with 900# rating are to be stress analyzed.
Also apply flange leakage screening for hydrogen lines.

special_service_override_rules.name: Sensitive equipment category mapping
warnings: Lines connected to sensitive equipment such as plate fin exchangers and cold boxes shall be checked as category B.

special_service_override_rules.name: Heater and steam generator process lines
warnings: All process lines to and from heaters and steam generators are included in critical category type K.

special_service_override_rules.name: Reboiler, flare header/sub-header, jacketed piping critical category
warnings: Reboiler piping, flare headers/sub-headers, and jacketed piping are critical category. Consult Piping Stress Engineer.
For flare lines above 24 inch, obtain maximum percentage of liquid flow from Process for weight calculations.
For jacketed piping, check core-jacket buckling stress and weld strength.

special_service_override_rules.name: IBR regulated line above 2 inch
warnings: Lines > 2 inch subjected to IBR regulations shall be Level 2 minimum.

special_service_override_rules.name: Stress analyst discretion
special_service_override_rules.condition_expression: Any line assessed by engineer as requiring calculation due to complexity, safety consequence, special load, vibration, questionable support, poor routing, or equipment sensitivity
warnings: The table does not exempt the engineer from making necessary calculations when assessed as necessary. Consult Stress Engineer.

special_service_override_rules.name: Two-phase slug or surge line above 2 inch
special_service_override_rules.condition_expression: nps_in > 2 and special_flags contains any of ['two_phase_flow', 'slug_flow', 'surge_flow']
warnings: Lines > 2 inch subjected to two-phase flow like slug or surge shall be Level 2 minimum.
Slug/surge loads and directions shall be shown on stress isometrics where applicable.

special_service_override_rules.name: Relief valve, rupture disc, ESDV, or block valve reaction possibility above 2 inch
special_service_override_rules.condition_expression: nps_in > 2 and special_flags contains any of ['psv_connected', 'relief_valve_connected', 'rupture_disc_connected', 'esdv_connected', 'block_valve_reaction_possible']
warnings: Lines > 2 inch connected to relief valves, rupture discs, ESDVs, or block valves with possible reaction force shall be Level 2 minimum.
PSV reaction forces shall be considered as per API RP 520 with dynamic load factor 2 where applicable.

special_service_override_rules.name: Low-temperature carbon steel below -45 degC
warnings: For LTCS material usage below -45 degC, impact test requirement shall be verified with Materials group. If ASME B31.3 Sec. 323.2.2 is used, separate stress-ratio verification is required.

design_condition_warning_rules.name: Bare line solar temperature
design_condition_warning_rules.condition_expression: special_flags contains 'bare_line' and design_temperature_c is missing_or_less_than_solar_basis
warnings: For bare lines, solar temperature of 65 degC should be accounted if not mentioned in line list.

design_condition_warning_rules.name: Steam-out temperature
warnings: All lines subjected to steam-out shall be designed at 120 degC minimum if not provided elsewhere.

design_condition_warning_rules.name: Bourdon effect
warnings: Bourdon effect, translations only, shall be considered for lines subjected to design pressure above 20 bar. This is a stress/flexibility modeling warning, not a pressure-thickness formula.

design_condition_warning_rules.name: Existing revamp line all cases corroded
warnings: For calculations involving existing revamp areas, all cases corroded shall be activated in existing lines.

design_condition_warning_rules.name: Sustained sagging limit
warnings: Sustained sagging limit is generally 10 mm, except steam lines limited to 5 mm and sloped lines as per required slope.

design_condition_warning_rules.name: Cyclic load count above 7000
warnings: Stress range factor shall be calculated for systems having operating cycles more than 7000 during design life.

design_condition_warning_rules.name: Large D/t ratio
warnings: For D/T >= 100, SIFs at bends and branches are to be evaluated and manually edited in CAESAR where applicable.

design_condition_warning_rules.name: Control valve reaction and actuator weight
warnings: Control valve forces shall be studied, especially pressure control valves, blowdown valves, and emergency shutdown valves. Control valve and actuator weights shall be obtained from vendor data.
Wind analysis required for column overhead lines, transfer lines, and flare headers.
Wind analysis required for line size 18 inch OD and above, including insulation if any, running above 10 m from HPP level.
Seismic analysis shall be done for lines 12 inch and above.

dynamic_analysis_rules.name: Slug flow

variables.alpha: bend angle or 90 degrees for tee

warnings: For slug flow, provide stop with hold-down at each change of direction, hold-down supports on vertical pipe parts, and maintain minimum natural frequency of 6 Hz.

dynamic_analysis_rules.name: Surge flow
warnings: Surge may occur due to pump start-up, sudden shutdown, or rapid valve closure. Static force analysis and modal analysis are required where applicable. Minimum natural frequency is 4 Hz.

dynamic_analysis_rules.name: PSV reaction
warnings: Safety valve reaction forces are calculated as per API RP 520. Dynamic load factor of 2 is considered.

dynamic_analysis_rules.name: Reciprocating equipment pulsation
warnings: Vendor shall perform analog/pulsation study. Piping shall be designed to avoid resonance with imposed dynamic loads.
Flange leak check required for 18 inch and above all lines except air and water lines.
Flange leak check required for hydrogen lines 4 inch and above with flange rating 600# and above.
Flange leak check required for 8 inch and above all lines with flange rating 900# and above.
Flange leak check required for critical piping circuits such as injection points and desuperheaters.
Flange leak check required for all flanges connected to inlet of PSVs.

equipment_interface_rules.name: Pressure vessels, columns, exchangers
warnings: Nozzle loads shall be lower than allowable reactions accepted by Mechanical Department. If allowable data are not available, WRC 107/WRC 297 or other approved local stress methods are required.

equipment_interface_rules.name: Air coolers
warnings: Air cooler nozzle reactions shall be checked as per API 661. Individual nozzle, header, and bay checks are required for critical nozzle connections.

equipment_interface_rules.name: Heaters and furnaces
warnings: Heater/furnace nozzle reactions shall be checked against API 560/vendor allowable loads. Heater transfer lines are vibration-prone; routing should be short and spring supports avoided where possible.

equipment_interface_rules.name: Reactors
warnings: Reactor connected piping shall include all possible operating modes, cyclic conditions, and reactor thermal growth.

equipment_interface_rules.name: Tanks
warnings: Tank nozzle flexibility and stresses shall consider API 650 Appendix P, radial movement, downward rotation, and vertical settlement.

equipment_interface_rules.name: Centrifugal pumps
warnings: Pump nozzle loads shall be checked as per API 610 where applicable. Flange alignment with spring hangers unlocked shall be checked. First rigid support from pump nozzle shall be adjustable.

equipment_interface_rules.name: Centrifugal compressors and turbines
warnings: Nozzle loads shall meet NEMA SM23 criteria/factor agreed with Mechanical specialist and vendor. Flange alignment is critical. Variable spring hanger variation shall be limited to 12 percent in these lines.

equipment_interface_rules.name: Packages
warnings: Package limits and nearby anchor feasibility shall be agreed with manufacturer. Stress model may need to include line inside package scope up to equipment.

level_1.user_message: Visual stress screening may be acceptable for this line, subject to routing/support adequacy and engineering approval. Minimum pressure thickness calculation may continue, but final pipe schedule must still satisfy project requirements.

level_2.user_message: Simplified CAESAR II stress analysis is required. Do not treat minimum pressure thickness as final pipe acceptability. Consult Piping Stress Engineer.

level_3.user_message: Detailed CAESAR II stress analysis is required. Consult Piping Stress Engineer before finalizing selected schedule, routing, supports, nozzle loads, or procurement thickness.

outside_scope.user_message: This stress-analysis specification does not calculate minimum pressure thickness. Use applicable piping code for thickness, and use this tool only for upfront stress-analysis criticality and warnings.
